title: GitHub Issues CLI commands (import/export)
description: Add `waystation gh import` and `waystation gh export` CLI commands as thin wrappers over the core gh module. Import pulls issues into the ledger; export pushes ledger issues to GitHub. Both support --repo, --json, and --force flags.
acceptance: `waystation gh import --repo owner/name` fetches issues from GitHub and writes them to the ledger. Outputs count + ids via CommandResult envelope.
`waystation gh export --repo owner/name` pushes ledger issues to GitHub. Creates new issues for records without an existing gh-{number} id. Updates existing issues matched by gh-{number} id.
Both commands support --json for machine-readable output and --force to overwrite existing ledger records (import) or ignore state mismatches (export).
Missing GITHUB_TOKEN produces a clear error message.
bun:test: CLI integration test calls gh import with mock server, verifies --json envelope.
typecheck, biome check, and waystation validate all clean.
notes: CLI pattern: program.command('gh').description('...') with subcommands. Token from process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN. Command flow: findProjectRoot() → core/gh.importExport(). The --repo flag is required. Repository must exist and be accessible with the token. CLI is a thin wrapper — all logic in core/gh.ts.
